title: capsella bursa-pastoris f. gracilis
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris f. gracilis, also known as shepherd's purse, is a subspecies of the Capsella bursa-pastoris plant, a member of the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antihemorrhagic effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing bleeding, treating wounds, and alleviating coughs and colds. The herbal preparation of shepherd's purse can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote healthy digestion and alleviate menstrual cramps.
